{{Infobox character
| name = King Zakareth VI
| aliases = King Zakareth
| children = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Leah]]}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, [[Talos|Heir Talos]]}}
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|wields the crimson Ragnarus blade}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|King}}{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|, Evening Star}}{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|, [[Ragnarus Incarnation]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|King}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Damasca]]}}
| appears_in = Traveler's Gate
| introduced = [[House of Blades (novel)|House of Blades]]
}}

''See also: [[King Zakareth VI Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] VI, also known as [[Zakareth the Sixth]], is the ruler of both [[Cana]] and [[Damasca]]. He holds several titles, including [[Lord]] of the Morning and Evening [[Star]] and the [[Incarnation of Ragnarus]]. A powerful warrior in his sixties with a muscular build, he is characterized by his gray hair and beard. His physical appearance is notable for having one bright blue eye and a second eye made of a glowing red stone from the [[Crimson Vault]] that replaced an eye lost to a scar. He typically wears dark metal armor reinforced with rubies, a black-and-gold peaked crown set with a large [[ruby]], and carries a spear and shield. [[Zakareth]] is a commanding figure who rules with absolute authority, expecting those under his command to govern in his name without question. His reign has lasted over twenty-four years. He is the father of [[Leah]], the [[Heiress]]. In his role as a leader and warrior, he has prepared for long-standing wars and is involved in complex political and supernatural conflicts involving [[Incarnations]], [[Travelers]], and the territories. He has been accused of significant injustices, including causing deaths and corruption, and is viewed by some as a threat to the safety of others.}}

== House of Blades ==
<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|The current reign is the 24th year of [[King Zakareth VI]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c17">''House of Blades'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|As the [[King of Damasca]] and father to [[Leah]], [[Zakareth]] VI rules from a throne carved from a single massive [[ruby]] outlined in gold. He is distinguished by his white hair and beard, a black-and-gold peaked crown set with a large [[ruby]], and armor of matching colors. His appearance is marked by heterochromia, possessing one bright blue eye while his left eye has been replaced by a red stone from the [[Crimson Vault]] that blazes like a star. Known as the Evening [[Star]], he maintains a [[vault]] containing a wand and has yet to name a successor. During his reign, he arrayed an enormous army against his enemies, planning to scour territory outposts before launching a full-scale attack on the city. Amidst these conflicts, he accused [[Simon]] of being a wanted criminal responsible for the deaths of fifty-three of his subjects.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">21</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King Zakareth VI]] is the [[King of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth's throne]] is carved from one enormous [[ruby]] with its shape outlined in gold.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is [[Leah's father]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has a bright blue right eye and his left eye was replaced by a bright red stone from the [[Crimson Vault]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] wears a black-and-gold peaked crown set with a huge [[ruby]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has a crimson eye that blazes like a star.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has a [[Vault]] containing a wand.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has not yet named a [[Successor]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] plans to scour [[Territory]] outposts and then attack the city in full force.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c6">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has white hair and a beard, a glowing red eye, and armor of black and gold.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] is a [[King]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has one blue eye and eyes that blaze red and ice-cold blue.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] claims [[Simon]] is a wanted criminal in his lands.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] accuses [[Simon]] of killing fifty-three of his subjects.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is a figure [[Alin]] imagines speaking like a king.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has one human eye.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] arrayed an enormous army against his enemies.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c17">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] wears armor.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] wears a crown with a [[ruby]] on the front.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c22" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has a blue eye.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is known as the Evening [[Star]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|The father of Heir [[Talos]], [[King]] [[Zakareth]] VI ruled from a [[ruby]] throne in [[Damasca]] within a throne room featuring doors etched with a bright red eye and a nine-pointed star. He carried a black-and-gold shield from his own territory and was seen watching destruction from the walls of [[Cana]] while discussing his hound. As the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]], he wielded the crimson [[Ragnarus blade]] and possessed skin patterns that shone with red and [[gold light]]. Although he was central to a setup involving the [[Endross Incarnation]], his true intentions remained a mystery to others. Ultimately, after being surprised by [[Simon]], [[Zakareth]] was blasted with [[Gold]] [[Light]] by [[Alin]] and killed.}}

<div class="wv-facts"><div class="wv-facts-head">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">17</span></div><div class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wv-facts-body">
<ul class="wv-fact-list">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is the father of Heir [[Talos]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c6">''City of Light'', chapter 6</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth's throne]] room doors are etched with a nine-pointed star and a bright red eye.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is resting against the throne.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] sits on a [[ruby]] throne in [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] brought up a black-and-gold shield from his [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has a throne room.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] stands on the walls of [[Cana]] watching the destruction.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] spoke about slipping the muzzle back over his hound.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Ornheim]] and [[Indirial]] need to reach [[King]] [[Zakareth]] so he can seal the Fangs in the [[Vault]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is mentioned in relation to a setup with the [[Endross Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]]'s real plans are unknown to the group.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] has patterns on his skin that shine red and gold.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] blasted [[King]] [[Zakareth]] with [[Gold]] [[Light]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Alin]] killed [[King]] [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] was surprised by [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] wields the crimson [[Ragnarus blade]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is the [[Ragnarus Incarnation]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
</ul>
</div></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|"Well, that was what King Zakareth would have said, he was sure."<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|“He has, at last, confronted King Zakareth, the Evening Star.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|“Do what you must,” King Zakareth said. “It’s time for me to slip the muzzle back over my hound.”<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
